skill.name: gpt-image-edit
skill.description: Use RunComfy CLI guidance to edit existing images with OpenAI GPT Image 2, including preservation prompts, multilingual text replacement, multi-reference composition, and output handling.
skill.summary: Edit existing images with GPT Image 2 through RunComfy CLI workflows.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: The static command, network, and filesystem findings are mostly true positives for the intended RunComfy workflow, not evidence of malware. The weak-crypto and path-traversal alerts are false positives from markdown text, links, and placeholder URLs. Publication is reasonable with a warning because the skill runs an external CLI, sends prompts and image URLs to RunComfy, stores an API token, and writes downloaded outputs.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill explicitly instructs agents to run RunComfy CLI commands. This is the intended function, but external command execution can run local tooling, depend on installed packages, and act with the user account permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly documented and are central to the skill. The risk is capability-based rather than malicious because the commands target a named model endpoint and use structured JSON input.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Third-Party Model API Use
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends prompts and image URLs to RunComfy model infrastructure and downloads generated outputs. This creates privacy and data-sharing risk for user-provided images, prompts, and referenced URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints and data flow are explicitly described in the skill. The endpoints match the advertised RunComfy service, so this is expected behavior with clear privacy implications.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Output File Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents token storage in a hidden home-directory config path and writes generated files to an output directory. Owner-only permissions reduce exposure, but token files and downloaded outputs remain filesystem-sensitive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and output-directory writes are directly stated. The skill also documents mode 0600 and an environment-variable alternative, which lowers but does not eliminate risk.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The traversal-related static hits come from a sibling skill link and placeholder HTTPS image URLs, not file reads or writes derived from untrusted path input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown navigation or example URLs. No evidence found of path concatenation, arbitrary file access, or traversal into local directories.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak-cryptography static hits do not point to cryptographic operations. The cited text is front matter description and an exit-code heading area.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, or signature verification logic in the cited lines. The skill file is documentation rather than executable cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE_CHECK: Review found no text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or attempts to override marketplace security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed security section describes normal privacy boundaries and does not contain evaluator-directed override language. No evidence found of prompt injection language elsewhere in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples for Model Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for installing or invoking RunComfy. These are expected for the integration, but users should review commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible examples in the documentation. They do not show obfuscation or arbitrary user-input shell interpolation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Image URLs and Output Downloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow sends public image URLs to RunComfy and downloads model outputs from RunComfy domains. This is expected but has privacy and filesystem implications.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill describes the outbound request, polling, and download whitelist. The behavior is transparent and tied to the advertised image-edit function.

content.user_title: Edit Images with GPT Image Edit
content.value_statement: Precise image edits often fail when prompts omit preservation details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured RunComfy guidance for GPT Image 2 edits.
actual_capabilities: Guides GPT Image 2 image-to-image edits through the RunComfy CLI.
Documents prompt patterns for preserving faces, products, brands, layout, and framing.
Shows how to replace in-image text, including multilingual scripts.
Supports examples for single-reference edits and multi-reference compositions.
Explains accepted size values, image URL limits, and common exit codes.
Describes privacy boundaries, token storage, outbound endpoints, and output downloads.
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account, CLI installation, and valid authentication token.
Input images must be publicly fetchable HTTPS URLs.
The model accepts up to 10 image references per request.
Long compound edits can drift and may need multiple passes.

use_cases.title: Localize Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Replace poster or ad headlines in another language while preserving the original photo, logo, layout, and style.

use_cases.title: Refine Product Visuals
use_cases.description: Swap backgrounds or adjust visual presentation while keeping the product identity and key brand details stable.

use_cases.title: Compose Reference-Based Scenes
use_cases.description: Combine a subject from one image with lighting, room, or color cues from other reference images.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Background Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-image-edit on this image URL: [URL]. Keep the main subject, pose, face, and brand mark unchanged. Replace only the background with [new background]. Use size auto and save the result to [output directory].

prompt_templates.title: Localized Text Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this poster with GPT Image Edit: [URL]. Keep the photograph, logo, layout, colors, and typography position unchanged. Replace only the headline with "[exact text]" in [language or script].

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced composition with two or more inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image 1 as the subject source: [URL 1]. Use image 2 for the environment: [URL 2]. Compose the subject into the environment, match lighting and color, and keep identity, clothing, and pose unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Production-Safe Edit Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a GPT Image Edit RunComfy request for these image URLs: [URLs]. Identify the primary image, preservation goals, exact edit scope, size setting, output directory, and privacy concerns before running the CLI.

output_examples.input: A product photo needs a cleaner studio background without changing the product.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a RunComfy edit request that keeps the product shape, label, and lighting stable while replacing only the background.

output_examples.input: A poster headline must be changed from English to Japanese.
output_examples.output: The assistant quotes the exact Japanese headline, names the script, preserves the layout, and routes the edit through GPT Image Edit.

output_examples.input: A subject from one reference should appear in the room from another reference.
output_examples.output: The assistant numbers the references, assigns subject and room roles, and asks the model to match lighting and color.

best_practices: Start prompts with the elements that must remain unchanged.
Quote any replacement text exactly and name the language or script.
Use size auto unless the edit intentionally changes framing.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many unrelated edits in one request.
Do not send private or restricted images as public URLs.
Do not omit the output directory or authentication setup.

faq.question: Does this skill edit images directly inside Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: No. It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to call the local RunComfy CLI for GPT Image 2 edits.

faq.question: What accounts or tools are required?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and either login state or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value.

faq.question: Can it preserve faces, logos, and layouts?
faq.answer: Yes, when the prompt states preservation goals clearly before describing the requested edit.

faq.question: Can it replace text inside images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends quoting the exact replacement text and naming the language or script.

faq.question: How many reference images can it use?
faq.answer: The documented endpoint supports up to 10 image URLs, with the first image treated as primary.

faq.question: What data leaves my machine?
faq.answer: Prompts and image URLs are sent to RunComfy. Generated outputs are downloaded from RunComfy domains.
